role: A layout-level view router that maps the current SurfaceState to the appropriate wheel-region visualization component and renders an empty-detail placeholder hint.
capabilities: Render explicit error and loading/no-project states for the wheel region
Route square view to the Grid component
Route circle view to Separator, Nucleation, or Wheel based on proj.binding.radius_from
Wire selection, poles, nucleation, and rung state into the active wheel component
Render a static SelectHint for the empty detail surface
substance_why: It implements real conditional routing across four visualization components and explicitly handles error, loading, and data states.
interactions_present: Selection onPick passed through to Grid/Wheel/Separator/Nucleation
Clear poles via onClearPoles in Separator
Set nucleation focus via setNuc in Nucleation
Set rung via setRung in Wheel
View mode and binding drive which component is rendered
interactions_missing: No retry or dismiss action in the error state
No keyboard navigation or focus management
No loading timeout or failure escalation
SelectHint is purely decorative with no interactive affordance
performance_risks: Inline arrow functions for onPick, onClearPoles, setNuc, and setRung are recreated on every render and passed as props
No memoization of the routed component or state-derived props
Frequent state changes may cause remounts of the active wheel component
reuse_why: The state-to-view routing rules and explicit honest-state handling are core domain logic, but the component should be restructured and memoized for the redesign.
decomposition_hints: Split WheelOrState from SelectHint into separate files
Extract the view-resolution switch into a dedicated router/hook
Separate error/loading state presentation from visualization routing
